data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification). One grounded coaching finding + affirmations. Coaching note, not a roadblock.

A T1 bright-line arguably fires (rollout/migration of a security/signing scheme), so prior-art consideration is in scope. The finding is T1(a): both halves of the Scope appear to ALREADY EXIST in this repo and in the parent epic 5fd2's settled design, so the plan risks re-building/repackaging shipped functionality rather than verifying+documenting it.

FINDING (T1(a) — prior art already in-repo; reframe build → verify/document + isolate the genuine residual):

- [ ] Reconcile this plan with the ALREADY-SHIPPED disable-config + preserved-and-ignored event rollout before treating either as net-new work — both Scope halves resolve to existing source and to epic 5fd2's settled decisions.
  - criteria: T1
  - location: plan Scope items 1 & 2 + Acceptance Criteria lines 1 & 2 (vs `src/rebar/config.py:260`, `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:651-662`, `docs/plan-review-gate.md:180`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:7-14`)
  - evidence:
    - Scope #1 / AC1 ("disable-the-gate config; gate off => claim behaves exactly as today; fail-open … covered by a test"): the flag ALREADY EXISTS — `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim: bool = False` (`config.py:260`); the claim path short-circuits to today's behavior when off (`transition.py:661-662`, `if not on: return None`) AND fails OPEN on an unreadable config (`transition.py:652-660`). It is DOCUMENTED at `docs/plan-review-gate.md:180` verbatim: "Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed." So AC1 appears already-satisfied.
    - Scope #2 / AC2 ("old+new coexistence … older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events"): this rollout pattern is ALREADY ESTABLISHED and the new events already adopt it — `sidecar.py:7-14` documents that `REVIEW_RESULT` is reducer-ignored, compaction-preserved, fsck-recognised, and "follows the preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (upgrade reconcile hosts first)." Epic 5fd2 child `db7b` already shipped this.
  - suggested_fix: Re-scope from "build a disable-config + a rollback path + coexistence" to "VERIFY and DOCUMENT the existing mechanism, and specify the genuine residual." The narrower residual the originating T12 finding actually points at is the deploy-ordering / cross-clone behavior of the SIGNATURE attestation event specifically (vs REVIEW_RESULT, already handled), and what happens to already-emitted attestation events on a rebar DOWNGRADE. State that explicitly and point AC1/AC2 at the existing `require_plan_review_for_claim` + `sidecar.py` rollout note rather than re-authoring them. If a DIFFERENT (deploy-time, not config-preference) kill-switch is intended, justify why the existing preference is insufficient — 5fd2 SC#4 explicitly decided "an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed," so a net-new mechanism must argue against that settled decision.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1):
- T1(b) anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three: PASS — no novel architectural pattern; the plan (re)uses rebar's existing config-preference + preserved-and-ignored event-rollout patterns.
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions: PASS — no "library supports X" claim; references resolve to real in-repo source (config.py, transition.py, sidecar.py).
